text: MARINES DEPLOYED: 2,200 Marines from 31st MEU ordered from Japan to Middle East on 3 warships (USS Tripoli, USS San Diego, USS New Orleans) with ~20 F-35Bs. Pentagon won't rule out ground operations.
HIGHEST VOLUME STRIKES: Hegseth confirms 'highest volume of strikes' over Iran — over 15,000 enemy targets hit total since Feb 28. Claims Iran's missile capacity down 90%, drone capacity down 95%.
BRENT ABOVE $103: Brent crude closes at $103.14/barrel (+2.67%), second consecutive close above $100. WTI at $98.71 (+3.11%). IEA reserve release and Russia sanctions waiver failing to contain surge.

RUSSIA SANCTIONS WAIVED: US Treasury waives Russian oil sanctions for 30 days (until April 11) to free stranded tankers and ease global supply crunch. EU condemns the move. Macron says Hormuz closure 'in no way justifies' lifting Russia sanctions.

LEBANON BRIDGE DESTROYED: Israel destroys Zrarieh Bridge over Litani River — first acknowledged strike on civilian infrastructure in Lebanon. Leaflets dropped over Beirut warn of 'Gaza-scale devastation.'

LEBANON TOLL SURGES: Israeli attacks kill 25+ in Beirut, southern and eastern Lebanon. Total Lebanon casualties now 773 killed, 1,933 wounded, 800,000+ displaced. Areas never before targeted hit for first time.

TRUMP ON KHAMENEI: Trump tells Fox News he thinks Mojtaba Khamenei is 'damaged, but probably alive in some form.' Urges Iranians to 'rise up' but admits overthrow a 'very big hurdle.' Says he'll know war is over when he feels it 'in my bones.'

KC-135 CREW DEAD: All 6 crew members of US Air Force KC-135 tanker killed in crash in western Iraq. Not hostile fire — possible mid-air collision with second tanker. Total US service members killed: 13.
GAS PRICES SURGE: US national average hits $3.63/gallon — up 35 cents in one week. California above $5/gallon. Analysts predict $4/gallon nationally by next week.
IRAN YUAN PLAN: Iran reportedly considering allowing oil tankers through Strait of Hormuz if trade is conducted in Chinese yuan — potential major geopolitical shift in global oil trade.

IRAN CEASEFIRE DEMAND: Iran tells mediators it will only agree to ceasefire if US guarantees neither America nor Israel will ever strike Iran again. Tehran also demands compensation payments and right to full nuclear fuel cycle.

FBI ALERT: FBI warns California police departments that Iran 'aspired to conduct a surprise attack using drones from an unidentified vessel off the US West Coast.' Oscars security elevated for March 15 ceremony.

SPAIN WITHDRAWS AMBASSADOR: Spain permanently withdraws its ambassador to Israel over opposition to Iran war and Gaza. Trump accuses Spain of being 'unfriendly' after Madrid denied US access to military bases for Iran operations.

GREECE PRICE CAPS: Greece caps fuel and food profit margins for 3 months to prevent price gouging from Iran war. Gas stations limited to 12-cent/liter markup. Germany limits gas station price changes to once per day.

JOINT ATTACK: Iran and Hezbollah launch coordinated 'joint and integrated operation' on Israel. Hezbollah fires 150+ rockets at northern Israel while Iran launches ballistic missiles at central Israel. IDF begins 'large-scale' strikes on Beirut suburbs.

IEA EMERGENCY: International Energy Agency approves record 400 million barrel emergency oil reserve release — largest coordinated drawdown in history. 60-day release to stabilize markets as Hormuz remains effectively closed.

IRAN 37TH WAVE: Iran launches super-heavy Khoramshahr ballistic missiles in largest attack wave since war began. Targets include Israel, US bases in Gulf, and Saudi Arabia. IDF reports multiple interceptions; debris causes casualties.
HORMUZ: 3 more commercial ships attacked near Strait of Hormuz — total 14 vessels damaged/sunk since war began. Shipping traffic down 95% from pre-war levels. 320+ vessels at anchor.
TRUMP: 'There's practically nothing left to target' in Iran. Claims campaign nearly complete. Netanyahu pushes for continued strikes. US-Israel strategic divergence widens further.
BREAKING: Six vessels attacked in Gulf & Strait of Hormuz — Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree hit by projectiles, 3 crew missing; Japan-flagged ONE Majesty damaged. Total ships struck now 16
DEVELOPING: Iran explosive-laden boats attack two fuel tankers in Iraqi waters — Safesea Vishnu & Zefyros ablaze, 1 crew killed, 25 evacuated. Iraq shuts down oil port operations
CYBER: Iran-linked hackers claim cyberattack on US medical tech company Stryker — first significant hack on American company since war began
THREAT: Iran publishes target list of US tech giant offices — Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Palantir, IBM, Oracle facilities in Israel & Gulf states threatened
STRIKE: Israel hits apartment in central Beirut's Aisha Bakkar area — seafront strike kills 7. Lebanon death toll rises to 634, 816K displaced
MARKETS: Oil surges — Brent $94.77/bbl, WTI $93.24/bbl after 6 vessels attacked. US Navy refuses merchant ship escort requests through Hormuz
MASSIVE BARRAGE: Hezbollah launches 200 rockets and 20 drones at northern Israel — largest attack since war began. Iran's IRGC calls it 'joint integrated operation' claiming 50+ targets hit
DIPLOMACY: Iran sets 3 conditions for peace — recognition of rights, war reparations, security guarantees. Russia providing tactical drone advice to Tehran
OIL CRISIS: Iraq shuts oil ports after tanker attacks. Brent crude surges to $95.79/bbl. Gulf states under sustained drone/missile attacks — Kuwait intercepted 8 drones
BREAKING: Mojtaba Khamenei issues first statement as Supreme Leader — vows Strait of Hormuz stays closed, Iran studying 'opening of other fronts'
OIL CRISIS: Brent crude surges past $100/barrel for first time since 2022. IEA releases record 400M barrels from emergency reserves. US contributing 172M from SPR
NETANYAHU holds first wartime press conference — says Israel 'crushing' Iran, calls Mojtaba Khamenei 'IRGC puppet.' Vows 'many surprises' in next phase
MARKETS: US stocks hit 2026 lows — Dow -739 points, S&P -1.52%, Nasdaq -1.78%. War economic impact deepening as oil crosses $100
HUMANITARIAN: UNHCR reports 3.2 million displaced across Iran. 200+ children among 1,348 killed. 77 healthcare facilities, 65 schools damaged
LEBANON: Israel expands operations past Litani to Zahrani River. Overnight strikes hit Beirut's Corniche evacuation zone — 8+ killed. Total Lebanon toll: 687 dead
DIVERGENCE: Trump says war ending 'soon' — Israel's Katz says 'no time limit.' Iran's Araghchi: 'We are not asking for ceasefire.' Larijani: 'We will not negotiate with the US'
CRASH: US KC-135 Stratotanker down in western Iraq near Jordanian border — 5 crew aboard, fate unknown. CENTCOM says NOT hostile fire. 4th manned US aircraft lost this month
KILLED: French WO Arnaud Frion killed in drone strike near Makhmour, Iraq — 5 wounded. First non-US NATO casualty in the Iran war. Macron: 'Unacceptable'
BARRAGE: Iran fires 'most intense operation' — Ghadr, Emad, Kheybar Shekan, Fattah missiles at Tel Aviv, Haifa, US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain. Israel says all intercepted
DIPLOMACY: Iran's President Pezeshkian sets 3 conditions for peace — recognition of rights, security guarantees, and reparations. First formal Iranian peace framework
MARKETS: Brent crude closes above $100/barrel for first time since Aug 2022. IEA announces largest-ever emergency release: 400M barrels from 32 countries. Dow -739 pts
CRISIS: WHO warns toxic 'black rain' containing benzene and toluene falling over Tehran from oil depot strikes — cancer risk for millions of residents
CONFIRMED: All 6 crew members of KC-135 Stratotanker killed in Iraq crash near Turaibil — possible mid-air collision with second KC-135. Total US military deaths at 13 since Feb 28
NATO intercepts THIRD Iranian ballistic missile fired toward Turkey — Adana residents woken by sirens at 3:25am near Incirlik Air Base
BREAKING: Explosion rocks Al-Quds Day march in Tehran — at least 1 killed as thousands rally in solidarity with Palestinians
Iranian missile strikes Zarzir in northern Israel — woman moderately wounded, teen girl killed running to shelter, 50+ injured by shrapnel and glass
Netanyahu in first press conference since war: Israel is 'crushing' Iran — mocks Mojtaba Khamenei for not showing his face publicly
Hegseth: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei 'likely disfigured' — every ballistic missile company in Iran destroyed, 15,000+ targets struck
DOMESTIC: Man rams vehicle into Michigan synagogue Temple Israel — suspect Ayman Mohamad Ghazali identified, relatives reportedly killed in Israeli strike
ENERGY: Treasury waives Russian oil sanctions for one month to free stranded tankers and ease global supply crunch amid Hormuz blockade
BREAKING: Trump announces massive Kharg Island bombing raid — 'obliterated every MILITARY target' on Iran's main oil export hub. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared — for now
ATTACK: Missile strikes helipad inside US Embassy compound in Baghdad — suicide drone targeted embassy radar first. IRGC claims '48th phase' of retaliatory campaign
BOUNTY: State Department announces $10 million reward for information on Mojtaba Khamenei and 7 other senior Iranian officials including IRGC commanders
NATO INTERCEPT: Turkey reports NATO defenses intercepted a 3rd Iranian missile entering Turkish airspace — residents of Adana near Incirlik airbase woken by sirens at 3:25 AM
WEAPONS: US Army deploys 10,000 AI-powered Merops interceptor drones to Middle East — $14K each, replacing $4M+ Patriot interceptors for counter-drone defense
HEGSETH: Iran's entire ballistic missile production capacity has been 'eliminated' — missile volume down 90%, drone volume down 95%. March 14 to see 'highest volume of strikes yet'
DIPLOMACY: UN Secretary-General Guterres arrives in Beirut for solidarity visit — says 'de-escalation and dialogue are the only way out' as Lebanon death toll reaches 773
IRAN THREATENS: Tehran warns it will strike oil companies with US connections if Kharg Island oil infrastructure is targeted — escalation warning after Trump's sparing of oil facilities
DAY 15: IRGC threatens UAE cities — warns US 'hideouts' are legitimate targets after Kharg Island strikes. Residents told to evacuate areas near ports and docks
STRIKES: Iran fires 3 ballistic missiles at central Israel — no casualties. Sirens sound across central Israel, smoke near Tel Aviv. Iran's missile capability down 90%+
DEPLOYMENT: 2,500 more Marines + USS Tripoli amphibious assault ship deploying to Middle East. Total US naval force now includes 3 carrier strike groups
ATTACK: Fujairah port (UAE) oil loading operations suspended after drone attack and fire — key alternative to Hormuz now disrupted
STRIKES: IDF destroys Iran's primary space research center and air defense factories in Tehran — 7,600+ Israeli strikes in Iran since Feb 28
COST: CSIS estimates US war costs at $16.5 billion through Day 12 — Pentagon told senators first 6 days cost $11.3B. Daily burn rate $891M-$1.4B
LEBANON: Israeli strikes killed 26 paramedics since March 2. Healthcare center in Borj Qalaouiye hit — 12 medical staff killed. UNIFIL HQ also struck
LOSS: KC-135 refueling plane crashes in western Iraq — all 6 US crew members killed. CENTCOM says not hostile fire; Iranian proxy claims responsibility
FRIENDLY FIRE: 3 F-15E Strike Eagles shot down over Kuwait in mistaken engagement — all 6 crew ejected safely. Pentagon investigating
DIPLOMACY: Trump rejects ceasefire talks — 'He's not interested right now.' Claims Iran 'about to surrender' on G7 call. Calls for international naval coalition
HORMUZ: Iran selectively reopens strait — allows Turkish ship, 2 Indian gas carriers, Saudi tanker. Remains 'prohibited' for US/Western allies
BACKCHANNEL: Iran intelligence ministry secretly contacts CIA through third country to discuss terms — NYT reports
LEBANON: Israel planning major ground invasion south of Litani River to dismantle Hezbollah. Lebanese government outlaws Hezbollah military activities
IEA: Record 400M barrel oil reserve release authorized — US contributing 172M from SPR. Prices remain above $100 despite release
POLL: 56% of Americans oppose the Iran war — NPR/PBS/Marist poll. Market stress approaching 'Liberation Day' tariff shock levels
CASUALTIES: US military deaths reach 13 — 7 by enemy fire, 6 in KC-135 crash. Iran: 1,444+ killed, 18,551 injured. Lebanon: 826+ killed, 2,009 wounded (Health Ministry)
KHARG ISLAND: US strikes 90 military targets on Iran's key oil export hub — oil infrastructure deliberately spared. Trump warns 'immediate reconsideration' if Iran blocks Hormuz shipping

IRAN RETALIATES: Missiles target central Israel, Eilat (child injured), and Jerusalem. Iran claims Khaibar missiles hit Netanyahu's office — no impact confirmed. Gulf states also targeted — Qatar intercepts 4 ballistic missiles

HEGSETH: Iran's military is 'destroyed' — missile capability reduced 90%, drones down 95%. Claims new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei 'wounded and likely disfigured.' State Dept offers $10M reward for Khamenei info
CYBER: Iran-linked hackers hit medical giant Stryker — employee phones wiped worldwide. 60+ hacktivist groups active. Poland reports possible Iranian hack on nuclear research center
IRAN RETALIATES: IRGC launches 10 hypersonic missiles and multiple drones at Al Dhafra Air Base in UAE — IRGC confirms targeting US forces stationed there
IRGC THREATENS NETANYAHU: 'We will pursue and kill this child-killer if he is still alive' — Iran's IRNA news agency reports official IRGC statement
HEAVY STRIKES: 20 explosions reported near Shiraz, heavy airstrikes on Isfahan area, southern Tehran, Dezful Air Base, Khomein, and Hamedan hit overnight
TRUMP ON DEAL: 'Iran wants to make a deal but I'm not ready — the terms aren't good enough yet' — NBC interview. Calls on allies to send warships to Hormuz
IRAN FM DEMANDS: Araghchi says war's end depends on guarantees attacks won't be repeated and compensation for damage caused during the conflict
CASUALTY UPDATE: Iran Health Ministry reports 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured since Feb 28. HRANA estimates true death toll may be 1,858+
LEBANON DEATH TOLL RISES: 826 killed including 106 children since March 2 — overnight strikes kill 4 more in southern Lebanon
OIL ABOVE $100: Brent crude at $103.14/bbl, WTI at $98.71 — second straight day above $100 as Hormuz crisis continues. Gold hits $5,032/oz
IRAN CLAIMS STRIKES IN ISRAEL: Army says drone attacks hit special police HQ and Gilat defence satellite communications centre
WASHINGTON POST: Two weeks into war, Trump 'knocked back on political heels' — 56% of Americans oppose the war, Democrats plan midterm strategy around conflict
IRGC CLAIMS IDF F-35I SHOT DOWN OVER TEHRAN — IDF confirms first stealth-vs-manned engagement, pilot recovered by CSAR team
US SUBMARINE SINKS IRANIAN WARSHIP IRIS DENA OFF SRI LANKA COAST — 87 crewmen killed, CENTCOM confirms engagement
PENTAGON IDENTIFIES 6 AIRMEN KILLED IN KC-135 CRASH IN IRAQ — total US military deaths in Iran war now 19
POPE LEO XIV ESCALATES LANGUAGE, DIRECTLY ADDRESSES WAR LEADERS: 'Cease fire!' — references school strike in appeal
IRAN FM ARAGHCHI: 'We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans' — rejects ceasefire overtures
HOUTHIS STAYING OUT OF IRAN WAR despite prior threats — weapons stockpile depleted, decision coordinated with Tehran
SEMAFOR: Israel 'running critically low' on ballistic missile interceptors — IDF denies, calls report 'complete fake'
Brent crude rises to $103.82/bbl, WTI $99.30 — Iranian supertanker pushes through Hormuz bound for China
BREAKING: IDF launches 'wide-scale' strikes on western Iran — 200+ targets hit across 20 attack waves, senior IRGC commanders Jalali-Nasab & Shariat eliminated
IRAN WARNS DUBAI AND DOHA RESIDENTS to evacuate specific neighborhoods — targets identified for potential retaliation strikes
Bnei Brak hit by Iranian cluster munitions — 3 wounded in first confirmed use of 'split-warhead' MIRV-type missiles against Israeli civilians
Baghdad Airport hit by 5 rockets — 5 wounded, US Embassy issues security alert urging American citizens to leave Iraq immediately
Italian MQ-9 Reaper drone destroyed by Iranian drone strike at Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait — $35M loss, no casualties reported
State Dept offers $10M bounty on Mojtaba Khamenei and 9 senior IRGC officials under Rewards for Justice program
IRGC vows to 'pursue and kill' Netanyahu — rumors of his death debunked by Israeli officials
Oil surges on Iran escalation: Brent $105.66/bbl (+2.4%), WTI crosses $101/bbl as markets react to IDF western Iran campaign
Energy Secretary Wright says Iran war will end in 'next few weeks' — signals administration confidence in campaign timeline
F1 cancels Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Grands Prix due to Middle East war — 2026 calendar reduced to 22 races
GROUND INVASION: IDF launches ground operation in southern Lebanon — enters town of Khiam in largest ground incursion since 2006. Multiple armored columns crossing border.
DUBAI AIRPORT HIT: Iranian drone strikes fuel storage tank at Dubai International Airport — massive fire forces flight suspensions for 7+ hours. World's busiest international hub disrupted.
KHAMENEI AIRCRAFT DESTROYED: IDF confirms destruction of late Supreme Leader Khamenei's personal aircraft at Tehran's Mehrabad Airport in precision strike.
FUJAIRAH HIT AGAIN: UAE's Fujairah oil hub struck by drones for 2nd consecutive day — oil loading operations suspended. Key bunkering port offline.
ABU DHABI CIVILIAN KILLED: Missile strike in Al Bahia district kills Palestinian civilian — total UAE deaths now 7 since conflict began.
SAUDI INTERCEPTS: Saudi Arabia intercepts 60+ Iranian drones overnight including 6 ballistic missiles targeting al-Kharj air base. Patriot and THAAD systems engaged.
IRGC THREAT: Iran's Revolutionary Guards declare USS Gerald Ford carrier support network — supply ships, tankers, escort vessels — as 'legitimate military targets.'
TRUMP NATO THREAT: Trump threatens NATO allies over Hormuz coalition participation, administration reportedly weighing seizure of Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal.
IRAN MISSILES: Iran fires 3 more missile salvos at central Israel — cluster munitions used again. IDF confirms multiple intercepts but some impacts in populated areas.
HORMUZ DIPLOMACY: EU's Kallas proposes Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement for Strait of Hormuz via UN — seeks international framework to guarantee shipping passage.
JAPAN OPTS OUT: Japanese PM Takaichi rules out sending warships to Strait of Hormuz coalition, citing constitutional constraints despite heavy oil dependence on Gulf imports.
RUSSIA PULLS OUT: Russia closes consulate in Isfahan due to ongoing strikes in the area — citing safety concerns for diplomatic staff amid intensifying Israeli bombardment.
ALLIES DECLINE: UK, Germany, France, Australia, and South Korea all refuse to send warships to Trump's Hormuz coalition. No country has publicly committed to joining escort mission.
NEW UAE EMIRATE HIT: Iranian drone strikes building in Umm Al Quwain, causing fire. No casualties. Fifth of seven UAE emirates now targeted since war began.

BAGHDAD AIRPORT HIT: Five rockets strike Baghdad International Airport — 4 airport employees and 1 engineer wounded. Second volley targets Victoria Base, former US facility providing logistical support.
IRAN MILITIA HQ STRUCK: Air raid hits PMF (Popular Mobilisation Forces) headquarters in Jurf al-Sakhar, Babil province, Iraq. At least 2 killed. Major blow to Iran's proxy infrastructure.

TRUMP OIL ORDER: Trump invokes Defense Production Act to restart California offshore oil pipeline (Santa Ynez platform) shut since 2015 spill. Could produce ~50,000 barrels/day. Governor Newsom vows legal fight.
IRGC BLUFF? IRGC spokesperson claims Iran has only used 'decade-old missiles' so far and newer weapons remain unused. Pentagon says Iran's military infrastructure heavily degraded.
IDF SPACE STRIKE: Israel destroys Iran's primary space research center in Tehran used to develop anti-satellite weapons and Chamran-1 satellite program
JERUSALEM HIT: Iranian missile fragments fall near Church of the Holy Sepulchre and National Library close to Knesset; 1 minor injury, 108 hospitalized in past 24h
ABU DHABI FATALITY: Missile strikes civilian vehicle in Al Bahyah area, killing one. UAE air defenses intercept 6 ballistic missiles and 21 drones on Day 17
BAGHDAD AIRPORT ATTACKED: 5 rockets hit Baghdad International Airport wounding 5; Kataib Hezbollah claims responsibility targeting Camp Victory
IDF MILESTONE: Israeli Air Force completes 400th strike wave in Iran — over 7,600 strikes conducted; IDF planning 3 more weeks of operations
ALLIES REFUSE HORMUZ: UK, Germany, Australia, Japan all decline Trump's request to send warships to Strait of Hormuz. No country has publicly committed naval forces
IRAN REJECTS CEASEFIRE: FM Araghchi declares 'We never asked for a ceasefire' and says Hormuz 'closed to our enemies.' Reveals Russia and China providing military cooperation
HORMUZ SHUTDOWN: Zero commercial vessel transits recorded through Strait of Hormuz on March 14 — first complete stoppage in history. ~400 vessels stuck in surrounding waters
OIL SURGE: Brent crude hits $102+/barrel, WTI near $99 as IEA calls Hormuz closure 'largest supply disruption in history.' IEA releasing 400M barrels from strategic reserves
POPE CALLS FOR CEASEFIRE: Pope Leo XIV issues strongest statement yet demanding immediate cease-fire. Vatican rejects 'preventive war' justification. Multiple US cardinals criticize war
